subject: Two large cylindrical towers overgrown with dense green and amber trees, seen from above, with birds in flight between them
description: The image is taken from a high aerial vantage looking down at two enormous cylindrical stone towers. Each tower is heavily colonized by large trees growing from its upper ledges and crown — lush green canopy with striking orange-red accent foliage. The towers themselves appear to be classical colonnaded structures, their stone now fully integrated with the plant growth. Small white birds — possibly doves or seagulls — are scattered in the space between and below the towers, visible against the shadowed greenery. The overall atmosphere is deep teal-green with warm amber accents from the trees. This is the Simic urban-jungle reclamation aesthetic made literal: nature consuming civic architecture.
foreground: tops of two colonnade towers with trees growing from their crowns
background: shadowed canopy and sky between towers, birds in flight
